```{r Setup, echo=FALSE}
############
# PACKAGES #
############

# BiocManager::install("SingleCellExperiment")
# install.packages("devtools")
# BiocManager::install("DropletUtils")
# devtools::install_github("immunogenomics/presto")
# BiocManager::install("scDblFinder")

library(DropletUtils)
library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(scater)
library(scran)
library(qs)
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(clustree)
library(harmony)
library(patchwork)
library(dplyr)
library(ggridges)
library(readr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(SingleR)
library(celldex)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
library(presto)
library(tidyverse)
library(tibble)
library(matrixStats)
library(viridis)
library(scuttle)
library(scDblFinder)
library(ggbeeswarm)
library(here)
library(scales)
library(Polychrome)
library(future)
library(glmGamPoi)
library(lisi)
library(pheatmap)
library(cluster)
library(ggrepel)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)


###########
# OPTIONS #
###########

options(
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  scipen = 999
)

options(future.globals.maxSize = 4 * 1024^3)  # 4 Go


plan(sequential)

#########
# PATHS #
#########

here::i_am("04_report/HD_vs_MM_scRNAseq_reanalysis.qmd")
# here::i_am("02_script/HD_vs_MM_scRNAseq_reanalysis.qmd")

# knitr::opts_knit$set(
#   root.dir = "~/Documents/Project/Thomas/20260116_scRNAseq_HD_MM_reanalysis")

seurat_path_Tcell_obj <- "/mnt/crct-share/crct13/PPA/PPA_work/project/01_HCvsMM/01A_HCvsMM_10x/results/Tcells_analysis/seurat_objects/QCed_seurat_Tcells_clean.qs"

# DIR_RAW    <- here("01_raw")
DIR_DATA   <- here("01_data")
# DIR_PROCESSED_DATA <- here("02_processed_data")
DIR_SCRIPT <- here("02_script")
DIR_RESULT <- here("03_result")
DIR_REPORT <- here("04_report")
DIR_ENV <- here("05_environment")
# DIR_QC     <- here("03_result", "QC")

dir.create(here(DIR_DATA, "Processed_data"),
           recursive = TRUE, 
           showWarnings = FALSE)



##########
# COLORS #
##########

# 22 col palette
pal <-c('#e6194B', '#3cb44b', '#ffe119', '#4363d8', '#f58231', '#911eb4', '#42d4f4', '#f032e6', '#bfef45', '#fabed4', '#9A6324', '#469990', '#dcbeff', '#fffac8', '#800000', '#aaffc3', '#808000', '#ffd8b1', '#000075', '#a9a9a9', '#ffffff', '#000000')

# N colors = N batches


# --- Sanity check ---
cat("Project root:", here(), "\n")
```

# Load data {.tabset}

```{r Load data}
seu <- qread(seurat_path_Tcell_obj)
```

# Normalization {.tabset}

## RNA matrix {.tabset}

```{r Normalize RNA}
DefaultAssay(seu) <- "RNA"
seu <- NormalizeData(seu, assay = "RNA")
```

## ADT matrix {.tabset}

```{r Normalize ADT}
DefaultAssay(seu) <- "ADT"
seu <- NormalizeData(seu, normalization.method = "CLR", margin = 2, assay = "ADT")
```

# Most Variable Features {.tabset}

## RNA matrix {.tabset}

```{r MVP RNA}
DefaultAssay(seu) <- "RNA"
seu <- FindVariableFeatures(seu, assay = "RNA")
```

## ADT matrix {.tabset}

```{r MVP ADT}
DefaultAssay(seu) <- "ADT"
VariableFeatures(seu) <- rownames(seu[["ADT"]])
```

# Scaling {.tabset}

## RNA matrix {.tabset}

```{r Scale RNA}
DefaultAssay(seu) <- "RNA"
seu <- ScaleData(seu, 
                 assay = "RNA",
                 vars.to.regress = c("nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"),
                 features = VariableFeatures(seu))
```

## ADT matrix {.tabset}

```{r Scale ADT}
DefaultAssay(seu) <- "ADT"
seu <- ScaleData(seu, assay = "ADT")
```

# PCA {.tabset}

## RNA matrix {.tabset}

```{r PCA RNA}
DefaultAssay(seu) <- "RNA"
seu <- RunPCA(seu, reduction.name = "pca")
ElbowPlot(seu, ndims = 50, reduction = "pca")
dir.create("03_result/PCA", showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave("03_result/PCA/RNA_PCA_elbowplot.png")
```

## ADT matrix {.tabset}

```{r PCA ADT}
DefaultAssay(seu) <- "ADT"
seu <- RunPCA(seu, reduction.name = "apca")
ElbowPlot(seu, ndims = 13, reduction = "apca")
dir.create("03_result/PCA", showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave("03_result/PCA/ADT_PCA_elbowplot.png")
```

# Batch correction (Harmony) {.tabset}

## RNA matrix {.tabset}

```{r Harmony RNA}
DefaultAssay(seu) <- "RNA"
#batch correction with Harmony (RNA)
seu <- RunHarmony(seu, 
                  group.by.vars = "id", 
                  plot_convergence = TRUE)

# Save convergence plot
output_dir <- "03_result/Harmony"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "/RNA_Harmony_convergence_plot.png"))
```

# Weighted Nearest Neighbor (WNN) graph {.tabset}

```{r WNN}
seu <- FindMultiModalNeighbors(seu,
                               reduction.list = list("harmony", "apca"),
                               dims.list = list(1:9, 1:13))
```

# UMAP {.tabset}


## Run {.tabset}

```{r Run UMAP}
seu <- RunUMAP(seu,
               nn.name = "weighted.nn",
               min.dist = 0.06,
               spread = 1.0,
               n.neighbors = 50,
               seed.use = 42)
```

## Visualization {.tabset}

```{r Viz UMAP}
# Visualization
metadata <- c("condition", "source", "id", "predicted.celltype.l1", "predicted.celltype.l2", "predicted.celltype.l3")

# Dossier pour sauvegarder
output_dir <- "03_result/UMAP/Metadata"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

for (meta in metadata) {
  p <- DimPlot(seu, reduction = "umap", group.by = meta) + ggtitle(meta)
  
  print(p)
  
  # Nom de fichier
  file_name <- paste0(output_dir, "/UMAP_metadata_", meta, ".png")

  ggsave(file_name, plot = p, width = 6, height = 5)
}
```

# Clustering {.tabset}

## Test several clustering resolutions {.tabset}

```{r FindClusters}
# # Clustering (commun RNA + ADT on WNN graph)
# Explore different resolutions
resolutions <- seq(0.01, 0.1, by = 0.01)
for (res in resolutions) {
  seu <- FindClusters(seu, graph.name = "wsnn", resolution = res, verbose = FALSE)
}
```

## Visualization {.tabset}

```{r Viz clusters (Clustree)}
clustree(seu, prefix = "wsnn_res.")

# Save the plot
dir.create("03_result/Clustering", showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave("03_result/Clustering/Clustree_WNN.png", width = 10, height = 8)
```

## Optimal resolution {.tabset}

```{r Choose opti res}
# Choose resolution 0.06
seu$seurat_clusters <- seu$wsnn_res.0.1

# Reorganize clusters names
seu$seurat_clusters <- factor(seu$seurat_clusters, 
                              levels = names(sort(table(seu$seurat_clusters), 
                                                  decreasing = TRUE)))
```

## Clusters on UMAP {.tabset}

```{r Clusters on UMAP}
DimPlot(seu, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_clusters") + ggtitle("seurat_clusters")

# Save the plot
ggsave("03_result/Clustering/UMAP_seurat_clusters.png")
ggsave("03_result/UMAP/Metadata/UMAP_seurat_clusters.pdf")

### !!!!!!! ADD A FACET VERSION !!!!!!! ###
DimPlot(seu, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_clusters") + ggtitle("seurat clusters") +
  facet_wrap(~seurat_clusters)

ggsave("03_result/Clustering/UMAP_seurat_clusters_facet.png")
ggsave("03_result/UMAP/Metadata/UMAP_seurat_clusters_facet.pdf")
```

!!!!!!! ADD A FACET VERSION !!!!!!! !!!!! and save !!!!!

## Clusters quality {.tabset}

### QC metrics per cluster {.tabset}

```{r QC metrics per clusters}
VlnPlot(
  seu,
  features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"),
  group.by = "seurat_clusters",
  pt.size = 0,
  ncol = 3
)

ggsave("03_result/Clustering/Clusters_QC_metrics.png")
```

nCount_RNA: Library size

nFeature_RNA: Library complexity

percent.mt: Mitochondrial content

### Libraries size per cluster {.tabset}

```{r Libsize per clusters}
# Plot number of cells per cluster with percentage labels of total cells
cell_counts <- seu@meta.data %>%
  count(seurat_clusters, name = "Cell_Count") %>%
  mutate(
    Percentage = round(100 * Cell_Count / sum(Cell_Count), 6),
    label = paste0(Percentage, "%")
  )


ggplot(cell_counts, aes(y = seurat_clusters, x = Cell_Count, fill = seurat_clusters)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Number (and %) of cells per cluster",
    x = "Number of cells",
    y = "Cluster"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Save the plot
ggsave("03_result/Clustering/Clusters_cell_counts.png")
```

## Cluster repartition per patients {.tabset}

```{r Clusters repartition per patients}
# Proportion of cells per cluster for each patient
cluster_patient <- seu@meta.data %>%
  group_by(id, seurat_clusters) %>%
  summarise(Cell_Count = n()) %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = Cell_Count / sum(Cell_Count) * 100)

ggplot(cluster_patient, aes(x = id, y = Percentage, fill = seurat_clusters)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Proportion of cells per cluster for each patient",
    x = "Patient ID",
    y = "Proportion of cells",
    fill = "Cluster"
  ) + 
  theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, 
                                 hjust = 1, 
                                 vjust = 1))

# Save the plot
ggsave("03_result/Clustering/Clusters_repartition_per_patient.png")
```

### Mitochondrial content per cluster {.tabset}

```{r Mito per clusters}
stats_mt <- seu@meta.data %>%
  group_by(seurat_clusters) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_pct_mt   = mean(percent.mt),
    median_pct_mt = median(percent.mt),
    sd_pct_mt     = sd(percent.mt)
  )

kable(stats_mt, format = "html") %>%
  kable_styling()

# Save the table
write.csv(stats_mt, 
          file = "03_result/Clustering/cluster_mitochondrial_content_stats.csv", 
          row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r Viz Mito per clusters}
ggplot(seu@meta.data,
       aes(
         x = percent.mt,
         y = seurat_clusters,
         fill = seurat_clusters
       )) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    alpha = 0.7,
    scale = 1.2,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    color = "white") +
  # theme_ridges() +
  # theme(
  #   legend.position = "none"
  # ) +
  # geom_vline(xintercept = 20, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    x = "% mitochondrial genes",
    y = "Clusters")

ggsave("03_result/Clustering/Clusters_mitochondrial_content_ridges.png")
```

## Remove bad clusters {.tabset}

```{r Remove bad clusters}
# Remove clusters 
seu <- subset(seu, 
              subset = !(seurat_clusters %in% c("8", "9","10")))

seu$seurat_clusters <- factor(seu$seurat_clusters)

```

```{r UMAP without bad clusters}
DimPlot(seu, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_clusters") + ggtitle("seurat clusters after removing bad clusters")

ggsave("03_result/Clustering/UMAP_seurat_clusters_after_removing_bad_clusters.png")

DimPlot(seu, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_clusters") + ggtitle("seurat clusters after removing bad clusters") +
  facet_wrap(~seurat_clusters, ncol = (length(unique(seu$seurat_clusters))/2) )

ggsave("03_result/Clustering/UMAP_seurat_clusters_after_removing_bad_clusters_facet.png")

### !!!!!!! ADD A FACET VERSION !!!!!!! ###
### !!!!! and save !!!!! ###
```

!!!!!!! ADD A FACET VERSION !!!!!!! !!!!! and save !!!!!

# Annotate clusters {.tabset}

## FeaturePlot {.tabset}

```{r FeaturePlot ADT}
dict <- list(
  "CD16-TotalSeqB" = "FCGR3A",
  "CD335-NKp46-TotalSeqB" = "NCR1",
  "CD197-CCR7-TotalSeqB" = "CCR7",
  "CD4-TotalSeqB" = "CD4",
  "CD8a-TotalSeqB" = "CD8A",
  "CD45RA-TotalSeqB" = "PTPRC",
  "CD62L-TotalSeqB" = "SELL",
  "CD127-IL-7Ra-TotalSeqB" = "IL7R",
  "CD25-TotalSeqB" = "IL2RA",
  "TIGIT-VSTM3-TotalSeqB" = "TIGIT",
  "CD226-DNAM-1-TotalSeqB" = "CD226",
  "CD38-TotalSeqB" = "CD38"
)

dir.create("03_result/FeaturePlot/Prot_RNA_Clusters_FeaturePlot", showWarnings = FALSE)

for (prot in names(dict)) {
  gene <- dict[[prot]]
  
  # ADT plot
  DefaultAssay(seu) <- "ADT"
  p1 <- FeaturePlot(seu, features = prot, reduction = "umap") + ggtitle(paste0(prot, " (ADT)"))
  
  # RNA plot
  DefaultAssay(seu) <- "RNA"
  # Vérifie que le gène existe dans RNA
  if (gene %in% rownames(seu[["RNA"]])) {
    p2 <- FeaturePlot(seu, features = gene, reduction = "umap") + ggtitle(paste0(gene, " (RNA)"))
  } else {
    p2 <- ggplot() + 
      ggtitle(paste0(gene, " (RNA) - Not found")) + 
      theme_void()
  }
  
  p3 <- DimPlot(seu, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_clusters") + ggtitle("seurat_clusters")
  
  # Combine plots
  p <- (p1 | p2) / p3
  p


  
  # Sauvegarde
  ggsave(filename = paste0("03_result/FeaturePlot/Prot_RNA_Clusters_FeaturePlot_", prot, "_", gene, "_featureplot.png"),
         plot = p, width = 12, height = 12)
}
```

# Clusters markers {.tabset}

## Find all markers {.tabset}

```{r FindAllMarkers}
markers <- FindAllMarkers(seu, only.pos = TRUE, min.pct = 0.25)

markers_filt <- markers %>%
  filter(
    p_val_adj < 0.05,
    avg_log2FC > 0.5,
    !grepl("^MT-|^RPL|^RPS", gene)
  )
```

## Top 10 markers per cluster {.tabset}

```{r Top10 markers}
genes.scaled <- rownames(seu[["RNA"]]@scale.data)

top10 <- markers_filt %>%
  filter(gene %in% genes.scaled) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(avg_log2FC, n = 10)

top10 %>% arrange(cluster, desc(avg_log2FC))
```

## Heatmap of markers per cluster {.tabset}

```{r Heatmap Top10}
genes <- unique(top10$gene)

# Extract scaled data for the selected genes
mat <- GetAssayData(
  seu,
  assay = "RNA",
  layer = "scale.data"
)[genes, ]

# Order cells by cluster
clusters <- seu$seurat_clusters
ord <- order(clusters)

mat <- mat[, ord]
clusters <- clusters[ord]

# Create annotation for clusters
cluster_ha <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  Cluster = clusters,
  col = list(
    Cluster = structure(
      Seurat::DiscretePalette(length(unique(clusters)), "polychrome"),
      names = levels(clusters)
    )
  )
)
# Create heatmap
heatmap <- Heatmap(
  mat,
  name = "Expression",
  top_annotation = cluster_ha,
  show_column_names = FALSE,
  show_row_names = TRUE,
  cluster_columns = FALSE,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 8),
  column_title = "Top 10 markers per cluster"
  )


# Save heatmap
dir.create("03_result/Heatmap", showWarnings = FALSE)
draw(heatmap)
png(
  filename = "03_result/Heatmap/Top10_Markers_Per_Cluster_Heatmap.png",
  width = 1200,
  height = 800
)



```

# Automatic annotation (SingleR) {.tabset}

## Make automatic predictions {.tabset}

```{r Run auto annot (SingleR)}
# Prepare SingleR input
sce <- as.SingleCellExperiment(seu, assay = "RNA")

# Reference dataset
ref <- fetchReference("monaco_immune", "2024-02-26")

# Run SingleR
predictions <- SingleR(
  test = sce,
  ref = ref,
  labels = ref$label.fine,
  assay.type.test = "logcounts")

# Add predictions to Seurat object
seu$predicted.celltype.l3_jordan <- predictions$pruned.labels
```

### Visualization of automatic annotations {.tabset}

```{r Viz auto Annot}
output_dir <- "03_result/Annotation/Automatic"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

p1 <- DimPlot(seu, reduction = "umap", group.by = "predicted.celltype.l3_jordan") + ggtitle("predicted.celltype.l1")
p1
ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "/UMAP_predicted_celltype_JD.png"), p1)

p2 <- DimPlot(seu, reduction = "umap", group.by = "predicted.celltype.l3_jordan") + ggtitle("predicted.celltype.l3_jordan") + 
  facet_wrap(~predicted.celltype.l3_jordan, ncol = 6)
p2
ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "/UMAP_predicted_celltype_JD_facet.png"), p2)

p3 <- p1 | p2
p3
ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "/UMAP_predicted_celltype_JD_combined.png"), p3, width = 12, height = 6)

```

# Manual annotation {.tabset}

## Create my celltype signatures {.tabset}

### Run presto to find markers {.tabset}

```{r Run presto}
expr_matrix <- as.matrix(seu@assays$RNA@data)
labels <- seu$seurat_clusters

presto_markers <- wilcoxauc(expr_matrix, labels)
```

### Construction of the signature table {.tabset}

```{r Construct signature tibble}
signature_tbl <- tribble(
  ~feature, ~cell_type, ~class,
  
  # Activated
  "CD69", "Activated", "State",
  "TNFRSF9", "Activated", "State",
  "IL2RA", "Activated", "State",

  # Apoptotic / stress
  "MALAT1", "Apoptotic", "State",
  "NEAT1", "Apoptotic", "State",

  # CD4 lineage
  "CD3D", "CD4", "Identity",
  "CD3E", "CD4", "Identity",
  "CD4",  "CD4", "Identity",
  "TRBC1","CD4", "Identity",

  # CD8 lineage
  "FCGR3A","CD8", "Identity",
  "CD3D", "CD8", "Identity",
  "CD3E", "CD8", "Identity",
  "CD8A", "CD8", "Identity",
  "CD8B", "CD8", "Identity",
  "TRBC1","CD8", "Identity",
```

### Visualization of all signatures together {.tabset}

```{r}
Tcell_signature <- "
^CD3D$|^CD3E$|^CD4$|^TRBC1$|
^FCGR3A$|^CD8A$|^CD8B$|^NCR1$|
^CX3CR1$|^IFNG$|^TBX21$|^CXCR3$|^STAT4$|^IL12RB2$|
^GATA3$|^CCR4$|^IL4$|^IL5$|^IL13$|
^RORC$|^IL17A$|^CCR6$|^IL17F$|^IL23R$|
^IL21$|^CXCR5$|^BCL6$|^PDCD1$|^ICOS$|
^FOXP3$|^IL2RA$|^CTLA4$|^TIGIT$|^IKZF2$|^CD27$|^IL7R$|
^PRF1$|^GZMB$|^GNLY$|^GZMH$|^CCL5$|^NKG7$|^KLRG1$|^GZMA$|
^LAG3$|^TOX$|^CD226$|^GZMK$|^HAVCR2$|^XCL1$|^XCL2$|^CD160$|
^B3GAT1$|^CD28$|^LAMP1$|^IL1A$|^FAS$|^TP53$|^TP53BP1$|^CDKN1A$|^CDKN2A$|^MKI67$|
^CD69$|^CXCR6$|^NR4A2$|^RUNX3$|^TNFRSF9$|
^CENPV$|^NCAPG$|^KIFC1$|^CENPE$|^ORC6$|
^CCR7$|^SELL$|^TCF7$|^LEF1$|^CD44$|^PTPRC$|
^MALAT1$|^NEAT1$|
^IFIT2$|^IFIT3$|^ISG15$|^STAT1$|^IFI6$|^IRF7$|^MX1$|^IFITM3$|
^TNFAIP3$|^NFKB1$|^NFKBIA$|^NFKBIZ$|
^HLA-DRA$|^HLA-DRB1$
"


selected_features <- grep(Tcell_signature, rownames(sce), value=TRUE)


m2 <- presto_markers %>%
  filter(feature %in% selected_features) %>%
  select(feature, group, avgExpr, pct_in) %>%
  left_join(signature_tbl, by = "feature") %>%
  mutate(signature_class = case_when(
  cell_type %in% c("CD4","CD8","Th1","Th2","Th17","Tfh","Treg") ~ "Identity",
  cell_type %in% c("Cytotoxic","IFN","Inflammatory","HLA") ~ "Function",
  cell_type %in% c("Naif","Memory","Activated","Exhausted",
                    "Resident","Prolif","Senescent","Apoptotic") ~ "State"
)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(cell_type)) %>%
  group_by(feature) %>%
  mutate(
    avgExpr = avgExpr - mean(avgExpr),
    avgExpr = scales::rescale(avgExpr, to = c(-2,2))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(m2, 
       aes(x = feature,
               y = fct_reorder(as.character(group), group),
               color = avgExpr,
               size = pct_in)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient2(low="blue", mid="white", high="red") +
  facet_wrap(~cell_type, scales="free", ncol=10) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "LT Compartment - All Signatures",
    x = "Genes markers",
    y = "Clusters"
  )

dir.create("03_result/Annotation/Manual", showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave(filename = "03_result/Annotation/Manual/Marker_Signatures_LT_Compartment_All_Signatures.png",
       width = 20, height = 10)
```

### View of specific signatures {.tabset}

```{r Viz specific signature}
m3 <- presto_markers %>%
  filter(feature %in% selected_features) %>%
  select(feature, group, avgExpr, pct_in) %>%
  left_join(signature_tbl, by = "feature") %>%
  filter(!is.na(cell_type)) %>%
  group_by(feature) %>%
  mutate(
    avgExpr = avgExpr - mean(avgExpr),
    avgExpr = scales::rescale(avgExpr, to = c(-2,2))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

for (i in unique(m3$class)) {
  p <- ggplot(m3 %>% filter(class == i), 
         aes(x=feature, y=fct_reorder(as.character(group), group), color = avgExpr, size = pct_in)) +
    geom_point()+
    scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid="white",high = "red")+
    # theme_minimal() +
    facet_wrap(~ cell_type, scales = "free", ncol = 4) +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1), 
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1)) +
    labs(
    title = paste0("LT Compartment - ", i),
    x = "Genes markers",
    y = "Clusters"
  )
  
  print(p)
  
  dir.create("03_result/Annotation/Manual", showWarnings = FALSE)
  ggsave(filename = paste0("03_result/Annotation/Manual/Marker_Signatures_LT_Compartment_", i, ".png"),
         plot = p, width = 16, height = 8)
}
```

For now I think that we don't need to be to specific on pop annotation because we have all Tcells. For now we have to identified major populations (CD4, CD8) by combining annotation from me + automatic + from PPA to be sure that we analyse the right populations later.

And then we extract count for specific pop cd8 and cd4 cells and perform a new clustering/annotation on each of them separately to have more precise subpopulations.

```{r Combine annot}
cluster_annotation_list <- list(
  "0" = "CD4_naive_and_memory",
  "1" = "CD8_Cytotox_exhausted",
  "2" = "CD4_inflammed",
  "3" = "CD8_cytotox_low_resident_inflammed",
  "4" = "CD8_naive",
  "5" = "???",
  "6" = "Dying_Tcells",
  "7" = "CD8_IFN"
)

# Add annotations to Seurat object
seu$manual_annotation <- plyr::mapvalues(
  seu$seurat_clusters,
  from = names(cluster_annotation_list),
  to = unlist(cluster_annotation_list)
)

# Names of cells with matching annotations between automatic (mine and PPA SingleR predictions) and manual (mine) annotation
## But first let's harmonize the names between predicted.celltype.l3_jordan and predicted.celltype.l3
seu$predicted.celltype.l3_jordan <- plyr::mapvalues(
  seu$predicted.celltype.l3_jordan,
  from = c("Non-Vd2 gd T cells", "Naive CD4 T cells", "Follicular helper T cells", "Terminal effector CD8 T cells", "Vd2 gd T cells", "MAIT cells", "Terminal effector CD4 T cells", "Th1/Th17 cells", "Th17 cells", "Th1 cells", "Th2 cells", "Effector memory CD8 T cells", "T regulatory cells", "Central memory CD8 T cells", "Naive CD8 T cells", "Natural killer cells", "Myeloid dendritic cells", "Progenitor cells"),
  to = c("Non-Vd2 gd T cells", "Naive CD4 T cells", "Follicular helper T cells CD4", "Terminal effector CD8 T cells", "Vd2 gd T cells", "MAIT cells", "Terminal effector CD4 T cells", "Th1/Th17 cells CD4", "Th17 cells CD4", "Th1 cells CD4", "Th2 cells CD4", "Effector memory CD8 T cells", "T regulatory cells CD4", "Central memory CD8 T cells", "Naive CD8 T cells", "Natural killer cells", "Myeloid dendritic cells", "Progenitor cells")
)



matched_cells <- WhichCells(
  seu,
  expression =
    (
      grepl("CD4", predicted.celltype.l3_jordan) &
      grepl("CD4", predicted.celltype.l1) &
      grepl("CD4", manual_annotation)
    ) |
    (
      grepl("CD8", predicted.celltype.l3_jordan) &
      grepl("CD8", predicted.celltype.l1) &
      grepl("CD8", manual_annotation)
    )
)
length(matched_cells)
length(seu@meta.data$manual_annotation)
# Le nombre de cellules avec annotation "Natural killer cells" dans predicted.celltype.l3_jordan
length(
  predicted_cells <- WhichCells(
    seu,
    expression = (predicted.celltype.l1 == "other T")
))

```

There is a lot of cells annotate gdTcells but I will not focus on them for now as I want to focus on CD4 and CD8 Tcells, and there are too different from cd4+ and cd8+.

# Specific analysis of CD8 Tcells {.tabset}

## Subset CD8 {.tabset}

```{r Cutoff CD8 subset Viz}
VlnPlot(seu, 
        features = "CD8a-TotalSeqB", 
        assay = "ADT", pt.size = 0, 
        group.by = "id") + 
  ggtitle("CD8a-TotalSeqB (ADT)") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 2, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")

VlnPlot(seu, 
        features = "CD8a-TotalSeqB", 
        assay = "ADT", pt.size = 0, 
        group.by = "predicted.celltype.l1") + 
  ggtitle("CD8a-TotalSeqB (ADT)") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 2, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

We have a bimodal distribution of CD4-TotalSeqB ADT, so we can use it to set a cutoff to separate CD8+ from CD4+ Tcells. We can also use the predicted.celltype.l1 annotation to help us to set this cutoff.

```{r Subset CD8+ cells}
# Subset CD8 Tcells
DefaultAssay(seu) <- "ADT"
seu$CD8_prot_pos <- seu@assays$ADT@data["CD8a-TotalSeqB", ] > 2

DefaultAssay(seu) <- "RNA"

seu_CD8 <- subset(
  seu,
  subset = CD8_prot_pos
)

print(paste0("There is ", length(Cells(seu_CD8)),  " CD8+ Tcells in the dataset."))
```

```{r Save CD8+ cells subset}
# Save qs object
qs::qsave(
  seu_CD8,
  file = file.path(
    DIR_DATA,
    "Processed_data",
    "HD_vs_MM_scRNAseq_reanalysis_SCE_CD8_subset.qs")
)
```

## Load CD8 {.tabset}

```{r Load CD8+ cells subset}
# Load qs object
seu_CD8 <- qs::qread(
  file.path(
    DIR_DATA,
    "Processed_data",
    "HD_vs_MM_scRNAseq_reanalysis_SCE_CD8_subset.qs")
)
```

## QC {.tabset}

### Calculations and vizualizations before cleaning {.tabset}

Maybe for each metrics, create a col in metatadata with status_metric (normal, low, high) based on 3\*MAD from median per emulsion AND another col based on isOutlier. Then perform cutting/cutoff based on these status cols. Non Stringent QC : remove cells that are outliers only in both of the methods ; Stringent QC : remove cells that are outliers in at least one of the methods.

And then Global Stringent QC : remove cells that are outliers in at least one of the methods for at least one of the metrics. Global Non Stringent QC : remove cells that are outliers only in both of the methods for all of the metrics (or critical metrics)

Maybe found critical metrics (not all metrics for QC stringence)

We use the function isOutlier from scuttle package to identify outliers for each metrics and for each emulsion. This function uses the median and the MAD to identify outliers, but it also takes into account the distribution of the data and can be more robust than a simple and global cutoff based on 3\*MAD.

Cut off/ OUTLIER DETECTION BY TECHNICAL BATCH (more qualitative QC than a global cut)

!!! Be careful to perform QC on counts slot (raw data) !!!

#### Cells count {.tabset}

##### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD4 Viz cell counts 1}
seu_CD4@meta.data %>%
```

#### Mitochondria {.tabset}

##### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD4 Run Mito}
# !!! Be careful to perform QC on counts slot (raw data) !!!

qc_mito <- perCellQCMetrics(
  seu_CD4@assays$RNA@counts,
  subsets = list(mito = grep("^MT-", rownames(seu_CD4@assays$RNA@counts), value = TRUE)))

seu_CD4 <- AddMetaData(seu_CD4, as.data.frame(qc_mito))


qc_mito2 <- data.frame(
  emulsion = seu_CD4@meta.data$emulsion,
  subsets_mito_percent = seu_CD4@meta.data$subsets_mito_percent
)

qc_mito2$high_mito_outliers <- isOutlier(
  seu_CD4@meta.data$subsets_mito_percent,
  nmads = 3,
  type = "higher"
)

qc_mito2 <- qc_mito2 %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    mito_status = dplyr::case_when(
      high_mito_outliers == TRUE ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!is.na(mito_status))

seu_CD4 <- AddMetaData(seu_CD4, qc_mito2$mito_status, col.name = "mito_status")


```

##### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD4 Viz Mito 1}
ggplot(seu_CD4@meta.data, aes(x = subsets_mito_percent, y = emulsion, fill = after_stat(x))) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis(
    alpha = 0.9,
    name = "subsets_mito_percent",
    option = "C",
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Mitochondrial content by emulsion",
    x = "Percent mitochondrial reads",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_QC, 
            "CD4_Tcells", 
            "Mitochondria",
    "Before_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD4_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Mitochondria",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "Ridgeplot_mitochondrial_content_by_emulsion_before_QC_CD4_Tcells.png"),
  width = 8,
  height = 5)
```

```{r QC CD4 Viz Mito 2}
)

ggsave(
```

```{r QC CD4 Viz Mito 3}

  geom_violin(fill = "grey90", color = NA) +
```

#### Ribosomes {.tabset}

##### Run {.tabset}

ADD AUTOMATIC MADS outliers by function isOutlier

```{r QC CD4 Run Ribo}
# !!! Be careful to perform QC on counts slot (raw data) !!!

qc_ribo <- perCellQCMetrics(
  seu_CD4@assays$RNA@counts,
  subsets = list(
    ribo = grep("^RPL|^RPS", rownames(seu_CD4@assays$RNA@counts), value = TRUE)))

seu_CD4 <- AddMetaData(seu_CD4, as.data.frame(qc_ribo))

qc_ribo2 <- data.frame(
  emulsion = seu_CD4@meta.data$emulsion,
  subsets_mito_percent = seu_CD4@meta.data$subsets_ribo_percent
)

qc_ribo2$high_ribo_outliers <- isOutlier(
  seu_CD4@meta.data$subsets_ribo_percent,
  nmads = 3,
  type = "higher"
)

qc_ribo2$lower_ribo_outliers <- isOutlier(
  seu_CD4@meta.data$subsets_ribo_percent,
  nmads = 3,
  type = "lower"
)

qc_ribo2 <- qc_ribo2 %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    ribo_status = dplyr::case_when(
      high_ribo_outliers == TRUE ~ "high",
      lower_ribo_outliers == TRUE ~ "low",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!is.na(ribo_status))

seu_CD4 <- AddMetaData(seu_CD4, qc_ribo2$ribo_status, col.name = "ribo_status")

```

##### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD4 Viz Ribo 1}

  geom_density_ridges_gradient(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis(
    alpha = 0.9,
    name = "subsets_ribo_percent",
    option = "C",
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Ribosomal content by emulsion",
    x = "Percent ribosomal reads",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
            "CD4_Tcells",
            "QC", 
            "Ribosomal",
            "Before_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
```

#### Library size (nCountRNA) {.tabset}

##### Run {.tabset}

Replace manual MADS by function isOutlier

```{r QC CD4 Run Libsize}
qc_lib_size <- seu_CD4@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    libsize_status = dplyr::case_when(
      nCount_RNA < median(nCount_RNA) - 3 * mad(nCount_RNA) ~ "low",
      nCount_RNA > median(nCount_RNA) + 3 * mad(nCount_RNA) ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(nCount_RNA > 0)


# qc_lib_size <- seu_CD4@meta.data %>%
#   group_by(emulsion) %>%
#   mutate(
#     libsize_status = dplyr::if_else(
#       isOutlier(nCount_RNA, nmads = 3, type = "both"),
#       "outlier",
#       "normal"
#     )
#   ) %>%
#   ungroup()


# Add libsize_status to metadata
seu_CD4 <- AddMetaData(seu_CD4, qc_lib_size$libsize_status, col.name = "libsize_status")
```

##### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD4 Viz Libsize 1}
ggplot(qc_lib_size, aes(x = nCount_RNA)) +
  geom_density(fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_rug(
    aes(color = libsize_status),
    alpha = 0.6,
    sides = "b"
  ) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      low = "blue",
      normal = "green",
      high = "red"
    )
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~emulsion) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    color = "Library size QC",
    x = "nCount_RNA (log10)",
    y = "Density"
  )

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
            "CD4_Tcells",
            "QC", 
            "Library_Size",
            "Before_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD4_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Library_Size",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "DensityPlot_library_size_density_by_emulsion.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

#### Librairy complexity {.tabset}

##### Run {.tabset}

```{r}
seu_CD4$log10_Complexity <- log10(seu_CD4$nFeature_RNA) / log10(seu_CD4$nCount_RNA)

qc_lib_complexity <- data.frame(
  emulsion = seu_CD4$emulsion,
  log10_Complexity = seu_CD4$log10_Complexity
)

qc_lib_complexity$low_complexity_outliers <- isOutlier(
  seu_CD4$log10_Complexity,
  nmads = 3,
  type = "lower"
)

qc_lib_complexity$high_complexity_outliers <- isOutlier(
  seu_CD4$log10_Complexity,
  nmads = 3,
  type = "higher"
)

qc_lib_complexity <- qc_lib_complexity %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    libcomplex_status = dplyr::case_when(
      low_complexity_outliers == TRUE ~ "low",
      high_complexity_outliers == TRUE ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!is.na(log10_Complexity))
  

low_quantil_complexity <- quantile(seu_CD4$log10_Complexity, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)

# Add libcomplex_status to metadata
seu_CD4 <- AddMetaData(seu_CD4, qc_lib_complexity$libcomplex_status, col.name = "libcomplex_status")
```

##### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD4 Viz Libcomplex 1}
ggplot(seu_CD4@meta.data, aes(x = log10_Complexity)) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightgreen", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = low_quantil_complexity, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Library complexity (log10GenesPerUMI)",
    subtitle = paste0("Red dashed line = 5% quantile (", round(low_quantil_complexity, 2), ")"),
    x = "log10GenesPerUMI"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
            "CD4_Tcells",
            "QC", 
            "Library_Complexity",
            "Before_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD4_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Library_Complexity",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "DensityPlot_library_complexity_before_QC_CD4_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD4 Viz Libcomplex 2}
# ggplot(seu_CD4@meta.data, aes(x = nCount_RNA, y = nFeature_RNA)) +
#   geom_point(
#     data = subset(seu_CD4@meta.data, !low_complexity_outliers),
#     color = "grey70",
#     alpha = 0.25,
#     size = 0.4
#   ) +
#   geom_point(
#     data = subset(seu_CD4@meta.data, low_complexity_outliers),
#     color = "blue3",
#     alpha = 0.7,
#     size = 0.6
#   ) +
#   geom_smooth(formula = y ~ x, method = "lm", color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
#   scale_x_log10() +
#   scale_y_log10() +
#   labs(
#     title = "Library complexity",
#     subtitle = "Genes detected vs UMIs (log–log)",
#     x = "UMIs per cell",
#     y = "Genes per cell"
#   ) +
#   theme_classic(base_size = 11)

ggplot(seu_CD4@meta.data, aes(x = nCount_RNA, y = nFeature_RNA, color = libcomplex_status)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(formula = y ~ x, method = "lm", color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("normal" = "grey70", "low" = "blue3", "high" = "red3")) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Library complexity",
    subtitle = "Genes(nFeature)/UMIs(nCount) per cell",
    x = "UMIs per cell",
    y = "Genes per cell",
    color = "Complexity status"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11)


ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD4_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Library_Complexity",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "ScatterPlot_library_complexity_before_QC_CD4_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD4 Viz Libcomplex 3}

       aes(x = log10_Complexity, fill = emulsion)) +

    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "red3"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ emulsion, ncol = 4) +
```

```{r QC CD4 Run cellcycle}
seu_CD4 <- CellCycleScoring(

  s.features = cc.genes$s.genes,
  g2m.features = cc.genes$g2m.genes,
  set.ident = FALSE
)
```

### Cutoff {.tabset}

```{r QC CD4 Cutoff Stringent QC}
seu_CD4@meta.data$Stringent_QC_status <- dplyr::case_when(
  # seu_CD4$DblFinder_class == "doublet" ~ "fail_doublet",
  (seu_CD4$mito_status == "high" | seu_CD4$libsize_status == "low" | seu_CD4$libdiv_status == "low" | seu_CD4$libcomplex_status == "low") ~ "fail",
  TRUE ~ "pass"
)

# Number of cells that fail QC
table(seu_CD4@meta.data$Stringent_QC_status)
```

```{r QC CD4 Cutoff Moderate QC}
seu_CD4@meta.data$Moderate_QC_status <- dplyr::case_when(
  # seu_CD4$DblFinder_class == "doublet" ~ "fail_doublet",
  (seu_CD4$mito_status == "high" | seu_CD4$libcomplex_status == "low") ~ "fail",
  TRUE ~ "pass"
)

# Number of cells that fail QC
table(seu_CD4@meta.data$Moderate_QC_status)
```

```{r QC CD4 Cutoff Non Stringent QC}
seu_CD4@meta.data$Non_stringent_QC_status <- dplyr::case_when(
  # seu_CD4$DblFinder_class == "doublet" ~ "fail_doublet",
  (seu_CD4$mito_status == "high" & seu_CD4$libsize_status == "low" & seu_CD4$libdiv_status == "low" & seu_CD4$libcomplex_status == "low") ~ "fail",
  TRUE ~ "pass"
)

# Number of cells that fail QC
table(seu_CD4@meta.data$Non_stringent_QC_status)
```

```{r QC CD4 Cutoff/Subset}
# Subset the Seurat object to keep only cells that pass QC
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- subset(seu_CD4, subset = Moderate_QC_status == "pass")
```

### Visualization AFTER cutoff {.tabset}

#### Cells count {.tabset}

```{r QC CD4 Viz cell counts After QC 1}
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data %>% 
  group_by(emulsion) %>%
  summarise(cell_count = n())
```

```{r QC CD4 Viz cell counts After QC 2}
features <- c("condition", "id")

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Cell_Counts", 
    "After_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)


for (feature in features) {
  p <- seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = .data[[feature]], fill = .data[[feature]])) +
    geom_bar() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal, drop = FALSE) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
    ) +
    ggtitle(paste("NCells -", feature)) +
    NoLegend()

  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
  plot = p,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Cell_Counts",
    "After_QC",
    paste0("Cell_Counts_By_", feature, "_After_QC_CD4_Tcells.png")),
  width = 6,
  height = 4)
}
```

#### Mitochondria {.tabset}

##### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD4 Run Mito After QC}
# !!! Be careful to perform QC on counts slot (raw data) !!!

qc_mito <- perCellQCMetrics(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@assays$RNA@counts,
  subsets = list(mito = grep("^MT-", rownames(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@assays$RNA@counts), value = TRUE)))

seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- AddMetaData(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, as.data.frame(qc_mito))


qc_mito2 <- data.frame(
  emulsion = seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data$emulsion,
  subsets_mito_percent = seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data$subsets_mito_percent
)

qc_mito2$high_mito_outliers <- isOutlier(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data$subsets_mito_percent,
  nmads = 3,
  type = "higher"
)

qc_mito2 <- qc_mito2 %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    mito_status = dplyr::case_when(
      high_mito_outliers == TRUE ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!is.na(mito_status))

seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- AddMetaData(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, qc_mito2$mito_status, col.name = "mito_status")


```

##### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD4 Viz Mito After QC 1}
ggplot(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data, aes(x = subsets_mito_percent, y = emulsion, fill = after_stat(x))) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis(
    alpha = 0.9,
    name = "subsets_mito_percent",
    option = "C",
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Mitochondrial content by emulsion",
    x = "Percent mitochondrial reads",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

dir.create(
  file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "QC", 
    "Mitochondria",
    "After_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Mitochondria",
    "After_QC", "Ridgeplot_mitochondrial_content_by_emulsion_after_QC_CD4_Tcells.png"),
  width = 8,
  height = 5)
```

```{r QC CD4 Viz Mito After QC 3}
    "ViolinPlot_after_QC_mito_percent_by_emulsion.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)

```

There are some cells with high mito content in some emulsions. We will remove these cells later

#### Ribosomes {.tabset}

##### Run {.tabset}

ADD AUTOMATIC MADS outliers by function isOutlier

```{r QC CD4 Run Ribo After QC}
# !!! Be careful to perform QC on counts slot (raw data) !!!

qc_ribo <- perCellQCMetrics(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@assays$RNA@counts,
  subsets = list(
    ribo = grep("^RPL|^RPS", rownames(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@assays$RNA@counts), value = TRUE)))

seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- AddMetaData(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, as.data.frame(qc_ribo))

qc_ribo2 <- data.frame(
  emulsion = seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data$emulsion,
  subsets_mito_percent = seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data$subsets_ribo_percent
)

qc_ribo2$high_ribo_outliers <- isOutlier(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data$subsets_ribo_percent,
  nmads = 3,
  type = "higher"
)

qc_ribo2$lower_ribo_outliers <- isOutlier(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data$subsets_ribo_percent,
  nmads = 3,
  type = "lower"
)

qc_ribo2 <- qc_ribo2 %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    ribo_status = dplyr::case_when(
      high_ribo_outliers == TRUE ~ "high",
      lower_ribo_outliers == TRUE ~ "low",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!is.na(ribo_status))

seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- AddMetaData(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, qc_ribo2$ribo_status, col.name = "ribo_status")

```

##### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD4 Viz Ribo After QC 1}
ggplot(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data, aes(x = subsets_ribo_percent, y = emulsion, fill = after_stat(x))) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis(
    alpha = 0.9,
    name = "subsets_ribo_percent",
    option = "C",
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Ribosomal content by emulsion",
    x = "Percent ribosomal reads",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

dir.create(
  file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Ribosomal",
    "After_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Ribosomal",
    "After_QC", 
    "Ridgeplot_ribosomal_content_by_emulsion_after_QC_CD4_Tcells.png"),
  width = 8,
  height = 5)
```

#### Library size (nCountRNA) {.tabset}

##### Run {.tabset}

Replace manual MADS by function isOutlier

```{r QC CD4 Run Libsize After QC}
qc_lib_size <- seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    libsize_status = dplyr::case_when(
      nCount_RNA < median(nCount_RNA) - 3 * mad(nCount_RNA) ~ "low",
      nCount_RNA > median(nCount_RNA) + 3 * mad(nCount_RNA) ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(nCount_RNA > 0)
```

#### Librairy complexity {.tabset}

##### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD4 Run Libcomplex After QC}
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$log10_Complexity <- log10(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$nFeature_RNA) / log10(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$nCount_RNA)

qc_lib_complexity <- data.frame(
  emulsion = seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$emulsion,
  log10_Complexity = seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$log10_Complexity
)

qc_lib_complexity$low_complexity_outliers <- isOutlier(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$log10_Complexity,
  nmads = 3,
  type = "lower"
)

qc_lib_complexity$high_complexity_outliers <- isOutlier(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$log10_Complexity,
  nmads = 3,
  type = "higher"
)

qc_lib_complexity <- qc_lib_complexity %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    libcomplex_status = dplyr::case_when(
      low_complexity_outliers == TRUE ~ "low",
      high_complexity_outliers == TRUE ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!is.na(log10_Complexity))
  

low_quantil_complexity <- quantile(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$log10_Complexity, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)

# Add libcomplex_status to metadata
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- AddMetaData(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, qc_lib_complexity$libcomplex_status, col.name = "libcomplex_status")
```

```{r QC CD4 Viz Libcomplex 1}
ggplot(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data, aes(x = log10_Complexity)) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightgreen", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = low_quantil_complexity, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Library complexity (log10GenesPerUMI)",
    subtitle = paste0("Red dashed line = 5% quantile (", round(low_quantil_complexity, 2), ")"),
    x = "log10GenesPerUMI"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```

```{r QC CD4 Run DblFinder}
sce <- scDblFinder(sce, samples = colData(sce)$emulsion)

seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$doublet_class <- sce$scDblFinder.class
```

## Normalization {.tabset}

### RNA matrix {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Normalize RNA}
DefaultAssay(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate) <- "RNA"

seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- SCTransform(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  vars.to.regress = c("subsets_mito_percent"),  # optionnal 
  variable.features.n = 3000, # To optimize and tune according PCA, UMAP, clustering results (see later)
  verbose = TRUE)
```

The SCTransform method performs normalization, variance stabilization, and feature selection in a single step. It models the gene expression using a regularized negative binomial regression, which accounts for technical noise and allows for more accurate identification of variable features. By regressing out the mitochondrial percentage, we can reduce the influence of mitochondrial gene expression on downstream analyses, which is often a source of technical variation in scRNA-seq data.

Don't hesitate to test ***different parameters*** (e.g., ***variable.features.n***) and evaluate their impact on downstream analyses (PCA, UMAP, clustering) to find the optimal settings for your dataset.

### ADT matrix {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Normalize ADT}
DefaultAssay(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate) <- "ADT"

seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- NormalizeData(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, 
  normalization.method = "CLR", # CLR = Centered Log-Ratio : standard for ADT (CITE-seq) data (Divide/Center each count by the geometric mean of the values in that cell, then log-transform)
  margin = 2, # Normalize across cells (so cols)
  assay = "ADT")

# Les counts ADT sont dominés par des rapports :
# efficacité de l’anticorps
# accessibilité de l’épitope
# capture / lavage
# bruit de fond
# 
# 👉 Si une cellule capte “2× plus d’anticorps”, toutes les protéines montent proportionnellement.
# 
# C’est un effet multiplicatif, pas une addition constante.
```

## Most Variable Features {.tabset}

### RNA matrix {.tabset}

```{r CD4 MVP RNA}
# DefaultAssay(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate) <- "RNA"
# seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- FindVariableFeatures(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, 
#                             assay = "RNA",
#                             selection.method = "vst",
#                             nfeatures = 2000)
```

Not nécessary as we used SCTransform for normalization, which already identifies variable features during the process and stores them in the "SCT" assay. Otherwise, run NormalizeData and then FindVariableFeatures (with specific argument/options) on the "RNA" assay.

SCTTransform is based on Binomial Neg and perform Pearson residuals to identify variable features, which is more robust and accurate than traditional methods (e.g., vst) for scRNA-seq data. It accounts for technical noise and allows for better identification of biologically relevant features, leading to improved downstream analyses (PCA, UMAP, clustering). By using the variable features identified by SCTransform, we can ensure that we are focusing on the most informative genes for our analysis.

### ADT matrix {.tabset}

```{r CD4 MVP ADT}
DefaultAssay(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate) <- "ADT"
VariableFeatures(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate) <- rownames(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate[["ADT"]])
```

We take all the ADT features as variable features because we have only 13 ADT features, and we want to keep all of them for downstream analysis (clustering, annotation, etc.). All are biologically relevant and we don't want to exclude any of them.

## Scaling {.tabset}

### RNA matrix {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Scale RNA}
DefaultAssay(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate) <- "RNA"
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- ScaleData(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  assay = "RNA",
  vars.to.regress = c("nCount_RNA", "subsets_mito_percent", "nFeature_RNA", "emulsion", "subsets_ribo_percent", "Phase"),
  features = VariableFeatures(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate))
```

We don't need to Scale data if we use SCTTRansform because it's already performed during the normalization step. It's still debate but run Scaling or not depends mostly on what we will see (OR NOT) later on PCA, UMAP, clustering results. If we see that the technical factors (e.g., nCount_RNA, subsets_mito_percent) are driving the separation of cells in PCA/UMAP, then we can try to regress them out during Scaling to reduce their influence on downstream analyses. Otherwise, if we see that the biological signal is strong and not confounded by technical factors, we can skip Scaling and directly run PCA/UMAP on the SCT assay.

### ADT matrix {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Scale ADT}
DefaultAssay(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate) <- "ADT"
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- ScaleData(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  assay = "ADT")
```

## PCA {.tabset}

### RNA matrix {.tabset}

#### Run

Run PCA on the "SCT" assay, which contains the normalized and scaled data from SCTransform. We will use the variable features identified by SCTransform for PCA, which are stored in the "SCT" assay. We can specify the number of principal components (npcs) to compute based on our dataset and downstream analysis needs. In this case, we will compute 50 PCs, but this can be adjusted based on the elbow plot and the amount of variance explained by the PCs.

```{r CD4 PCA RNA Run}
DefaultAssay(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate) <- "SCT"
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- RunPCA(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, 
  reduction.name = "pca",
  npcs = 50,
  weight.by.var = TRUE,
  verbose = TRUE)
```

#### Visualize

```{r CD4 PCA RNA ElbowPlot}
p_pca_rna <- ElbowPlot(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, 
  ndims = 50, 
  reduction = "pca") +
  ggtitle("PCA Elbow Plot - RNA assay")
p_pca_rna

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
            "CD4_Tcells",
            "PCA",
            "RNA"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  plot = p_pca_rna,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "PCA",
    "RNA",
    "RNA_PCA_elbowplot.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

15 PCs seems to be a good choice based on the elbow plot, but we can also look at the percentage of variance explained by each PC to make a more informed decision. We can also test different numbers of PCs (e.g., 10, 15, 20) and evaluate their impact on downstream analyses (UMAP, clustering) to find the optimal number of PCs for our dataset.

```{r CD4 PCA RNA Variance Explained}
# Percentage of variance explained by each PC
pct_var <- seu_CD4_QC_Moderate[["pca"]]@stdev^2
pct_var <- pct_var / sum(pct_var) * 100 

cum_var <- cumsum(pct_var)


df_var <- data.frame(
  PC = 1:length(pct_var),
  Variance = pct_var,
  CumulativeVariance = cum_var
)

p_cumvar_rna <- ggplot(df_var, aes(x = PC)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = Variance), stat = "identity", fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_line(aes(y = CumulativeVariance), color = "red", size = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(y = CumulativeVariance), color = "red", size = 2) +
  ylab("Variance Explained (%)") +
  xlab("Principal Components") +
  ggtitle("Explained variance per PC with cumulative variance") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:length(pct_var)) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)
  )
p_cumvar_rna

ggsave(
  plot = p_cumvar_rna,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "PCA",
    "RNA",
    "RNA_Variance_explained_by_PCs.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

15 PC allow us to explained almost 75% of the variance in RNA matrix

```{r CD4 PCA RNA PC Heatmap}
DefaultAssay(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate) <- "SCT"

p_pca_rna_heatmap <- DimHeatmap(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  reduction = "pca",
  dims = 1:15,
  cells = 500,
  balanced = TRUE,
  fast = TRUE
) + ggtitle("PCA Heatmap - RNA assay")
p_pca_rna_heatmap

ggsave(
  plot = p_pca_rna_heatmap,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "PCA",
    "RNA",
    "RNA_PCA_heatmap.png"),
)
```

Heatmap of the top genes contributing to the first 15 PCs, which can help us understand the biological processes driving the variation in our dataset. We can look at the top genes for each PC and see if they are associated with specific pathways or cell types, which can provide insights into the underlying biology of our CD4 T cells.

```{r CD4 PCA DimPlot RNA emulsion}
p_pca_rna_dimplot_emulsion <- DimPlot(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  reduction = "pca",
  group.by = "emulsion") +
  ggtitle("PCA DimPlot - RNA assay")
p_pca_rna_dimplot_emulsion

ggsave(
  plot = p_pca_rna_dimplot_emulsion,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "PCA",
    "RNA",
    "RNA_PCA_DimPlot_by_emulsion.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)

p_pca_rna_dimplot_emulsion_wrap <- DimPlot(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  reduction = "pca",
  group.by = "emulsion") +
  ggtitle("PCA DimPlot - RNA assay") + 
  facet_wrap(~emulsion, ncol = 6)
p_pca_rna_dimplot_emulsion_wrap

ggsave(
  plot = p_pca_rna_dimplot_emulsion_wrap,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "PCA",
    "RNA",
    "RNA_PCA_DimPlot_by_emulsion_wrap.png"),
  width = 15,
  height = 10)
```

There is some separation of cells based on emulsion in PCA space, which suggests that there may be some batch effect or technical variation associated with the emulsion. This could be due to differences in sample processing, library preparation, or sequencing between emulsions. It's important to keep this in mind when interpreting downstream analyses (e.g., clustering, differential expression) and consider applying batch correction methods (e.g., Harmony) if necessary to mitigate the influence of this technical variation.

```{r CD4 PCA DimPlot RNA Phase}
p_pca_rna_dimplot_phase <- DimPlot(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  reduction = "pca",
  group.by = "Phase") +
  ggtitle("PCA DimPlot - RNA assay")
p_pca_rna_dimplot_phase

ggsave(
  plot = p_pca_rna_dimplot_phase,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "PCA",
    "RNA",
    "RNA_PCA_DimPlot_by_Phase.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)

p_pca_rna_dimplot_phase_wrap <- DimPlot(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  reduction = "pca",
  group.by = "Phase") +
  ggtitle("PCA DimPlot - RNA assay") + 
  facet_wrap(~Phase)
p_pca_rna_dimplot_phase_wrap

ggsave(
  plot = p_pca_rna_dimplot_phase_wrap,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "PCA",
    "RNA",
    "RNA_PCA_DimPlot_by_Phase_wrap.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

No clear separation of cells based on cell cycle phase in PCA space, which is good because it means that the biological factors (e.g., cell cycle phase) are not driving the separation of cells in PCA space. This suggests that the normalization and scaling steps have successfully mitigated the influence of these factors on downstream analyses.

We take ALL !!!

## Batch correction (Harmony) {.tabset}

### RNA matrix {.tabset}

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Harmony RNA Run}
DefaultAssay(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate) <- "SCT"
#batch correction with Harmony (RNA)
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- RunHarmony(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
                            group.by.vars = "id",
                            plot_convergence = TRUE,
                            max.iter = 20,
                            reduction.save = "harmony",
                            assay.use = "SCT",
                            verbose = TRUE,
                            theta = 2, # theta is the diversity clustering penalty param
                            lambda = 0.1, # lambda is the ridge regression penalty param
                            sigma = 0.1) # sigma is the bandwidth parameter for soft k-means clustering (default 0.1, smaller values lead to more aggressive correction)
```

theta is the diversity clustering penalty parameter, which controls the strength of batch correction. Higher values will result in stronger correction, while lower values will allow for more batch-specific variation to remain in the data. The optimal value of theta can depend on the specific dataset and the degree of batch effect present, so it may require some tuning to find the best balance between correcting for batch effects and preserving biological variation.

Ridge regression penalty. Default lambda=1. Bigger values protect against over correction. If several covariates are specified, then lambda can also be a vector which needs to be equal length with the number of variables to be corrected. In this scenario, each covariate level group will be assigned the scalars specified by the user. If set to NULL, harmony will start lambda estimation mode to determine lambdas automatically and try to minimize overcorrection (Use with caution still in beta testing).

Width of soft kmeans clusters. Default sigma=0.1. Sigma scales the distance from a cell to cluster centroids. Larger values of sigma result in cells assigned to more clusters. Smaller values of sigma make soft kmeans cluster approach hard clustering.

#### Visualize {.tabset}

##### Convergence {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Harmony RNA Convergence}
p_converge_Harmony <- last_plot()

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
            "CD4_Tcells",
            "Harmony"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  plot = p_converge_Harmony,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Harmony",
    "CD4_RNA_Harmony_convergence_plot.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

Convergence plot of the Harmony algorithm, which shows how the batch correction process is progressing over iterations. The plot typically displays the objective function value (or a similar metric) on the y-axis and the iteration number on the x-axis. A decreasing trend in the objective function value indicates that the algorithm is successfully correcting for batch effects and converging towards a solution. If the plot shows a plateau or an increase in the objective function value, it may suggest that the algorithm is not converging properly, and you may need to adjust parameters (e.g., theta, lambda, sigma) or increase the number of iterations to achieve better batch correction. Here we are good.

##### ElbowPlot {.tabset}

```{r}
p_elbow_Harmony <- ElbowPlot(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, 
  ndims = 50, 
  reduction = "harmony") +
  ggtitle("PCA Elbow Plot after Harmony - RNA assay")
p_elbow_Harmony

ggsave(
  plot = p_elbow_Harmony,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Harmony",
    "CD4_RNA_Harmony_PCA_elbowplot.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

We have the almost same number of PCs to take into account after Harmony correction, which is good because it means that the batch correction process has not significantly altered the structure of the data in PCA space. This suggests that the Harmony algorithm has successfully corrected for batch effects while preserving the underlying biological variation in our CD4 T cell dataset.

We will take 16 PC post-harmony

##### PCA after Harmony {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Harmony RNA DimPlot PCA}
p_pca_harmony <- DimPlot(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  reduction = "harmony",
  group.by = "emulsion") +
  ggtitle("PCA DimPlot after Harmony - RNA assay")
p_pca_harmony

ggsave(

    "CD4_Tcells",

    "CD4_RNA_Harmony_PCA_DimPlot_by_emulsion.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)

p_pca_harmony_wrap <- DimPlot(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  reduction = "harmony",
  group.by = "emulsion") +
  ggtitle("PCA DimPlot after Harmony - RNA assay") +
  facet_wrap(~emulsion, ncol = 6)
p_pca_harmony_wrap

ggsave(
  plot = p_pca_harmony_wrap,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Harmony",
    "CD4_RNA_Harmony_PCA_DimPlot_by_emulsion_wrap.png"),
  width = 15,
  height = 10)
```

##### LISI score after Harmony {.tabset}

###### Run

```{r CD4 LISI score Run}
emb <- Embeddings(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, reduction = "harmony")
meta <- seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data

lisi_scores <- compute_lisi(
  emb,
  meta,
  c("emulsion", "source", "condition")
)

summary(lisi_scores)

```

emulsion_LISI quantifies batch mixing across the embedding: higher values indicate better batch correction.

source_LISI & condition_LISI quantifies condition mixing: lower values indicate better preservation of biological separation between conditions.

An optimal integration is therefore characterized by high emulsion_LISI and low condition_LISI & source_LISI, indicating that technical batch effects are removed while biological differences between HH and MM are preserved.

We have successfully corrected for batch effects (high emulsion_LISI) but we also see some loss of biological separation between conditions (lower condition_LISI) and sources (lower source_LISI), which suggests that the Harmony algorithm may have overcorrected in some cases, leading to a reduction in the biological signal. This is a common trade-off in batch correction, and it highlights the importance of carefully tuning parameters (e.g., theta, lambda, sigma) to find the right balance between correcting for batch effects and preserving biological variation in our CD4 T cell dataset.

###### Visualize

```{r CD4 LISI score Viz}
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$emulsion_LISI  <- lisi_scores$emulsion
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$source_LISI    <- lisi_scores$source
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$condition_LISI <- lisi_scores$condition

FeaturePlot(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  features = c("emulsion_LISI", "source_LISI", "condition_LISI"),
  cols = c("blue", "yellow", "red"),
  min.cutoff = "q05",
  max.cutoff = "q95"
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Harmony",
    "CD4_RNA_Harmony_LISI_scores.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)

```

```{r CD4 LISI score Viz 2}
ggplot(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data,
       aes(x = predicted.celltype.l2, y = condition_LISI)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.3) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_classic()

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Harmony",
    "CD4_RNA_Harmony_LISI_scores_by_celltype.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r}
emb_raw <- Embeddings(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, reduction = "pca")
emb_int <- Embeddings(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, reduction = "harmony")

lisi_raw  <- compute_lisi(emb_raw,  meta, c("emulsion", "condition"))
lisi_int  <- compute_lisi(emb_int,  meta, c("emulsion", "condition"))

df <- rbind(
  data.frame(score = lisi_raw$emulsion, type = "Raw", var = "Batch"),
  data.frame(score = lisi_int$emulsion, type = "Integrated", var = "Batch"),
  data.frame(score = lisi_raw$condition, type = "Raw", var = "Condition"),
  data.frame(score = lisi_int$condition, type = "Integrated", var = "Condition")
)

ggplot(df, aes(type, score, fill = type)) +
  geom_violin(trim = TRUE) +
  facet_wrap(~var, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_classic() +
  NoLegend() +
  ggtitle("LISI scores before and after Harmony correction") +
  labs(y = "LISI Score (Mixing/Integration)")

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Harmony",
    "CD4_RNA_Harmony_LISI_scores_comparison.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

We can see that the emulsion_LISI score has significantly increased after Harmony correction, indicating improved batch mixing and successful correction of batch effects. However, the condition_LISI score has also increased, suggesting that some biological separation between conditions may have been lost during the batch correction process.

## Weighted Nearest Neighbor (WNN) graph {.tabset}

```{r CD4 WNN}
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- FindMultiModalNeighbors(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
                               reduction.list = list("harmony", "apca"),
                               dims.list = list(1:16, 1:13),
                               k.nn = 20,
                               verbose = TRUE,)
```

### Run {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Run UMAP}
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- RunUMAP(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  nn.name = "weighted.nn",
  min.dist = 0.05,
  spread = 2.0,
  n.neighbors = 40,
  seed.use = 42)
```

### Visualization {.tabset}

Add density lines on UMAP to better visualize the distribution of cells in UMAP space and identify potential clusters or subpopulations. Density lines can help highlight areas of high cell density, which may correspond to distinct cell types or states. This can be particularly useful when visualizing complex datasets with many cells, as it can provide additional insights into the structure of the data and help guide downstream analyses (e.g., clustering, differential expression).

```{r CD4 UMAP Viz 1}
# Visualization
metadata <- c("condition", 
              "source", 
              "id", 
              "predicted.celltype.l1", 
              "predicted.celltype.l2", 
              "predicted.celltype.l3")

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
            "CD4_Tcells",
            "UMAP",
            "Metadata"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)
  
for (meta in metadata) {
  p <- DimPlot(
    seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, 
    reduction = "umap", 
    group.by = meta) + 
    ggtitle(meta)
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
    plot = p,
    filename = file.path(
      DIR_RESULT,
      "CD4_Tcells",
      "UMAP",
      "Metadata",
      paste0("UMAP_metadata_", meta, ".png")),
    width = 12,
    height = 6)
}
```

```{r CD4 UMAP Viz 2}
# Visualization
metadata <- c("condition", 
              "source", 
              "predicted.celltype.l1")

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
            "CD4_Tcells",
            "UMAP",
            "Metadata"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

for (meta in metadata) {
  p <- DimPlot(
    seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, 
    reduction = "umap", 
    group.by = meta,
    split.by = meta) + 
    ggtitle(meta)
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
    plot = p,
    filename = file.path(
      DIR_RESULT,

    width = 12,
    height = 6)
}
```

```{r CD4 UMAP Viz 3}
df_umap <- as.data.frame(Embeddings(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, "umap")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(id = seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data$id,
                celltype = seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data$predicted.celltype.l2,
                condition = seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data$condition)


ggplot(df_umap, aes(x = umap_1, y = umap_2, color = condition)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ id, scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "UMAP per patient (id)", color = "Cell type") +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 8))

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "UMAP",
    "Metadata",
    "UMAP_by_id.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

## Clustering {.tabset}

Maybe try also to cluster with SCE to see if we can get better clusters Maybe try also other algorithms (e.g., Leiden) to see if we can get better clusters

### Test several clustering resolutions {.tabset}

```{r CD4 FindClusters}
# # Clustering (commun RNA + ADT on WNN graph)
# Explore different resolutions

# remove previous clustering results if exist


resolutions <- seq(0.01, 0.5, by = 0.01)
for (res in resolutions) {
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- FindClusters(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, graph.name = "wsnn", resolution = res, verbose = FALSE)
}
```

### Visualization {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Viz clusters (Clustree)}
clustree(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, prefix = "wsnn_res.")

# Save the plot
dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
            "CD4_Tcells",
            "Clustering"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Clustering",
    "Clustree_WNN.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 12)
```

### Optimal resolution {.tabset}

Sur-clustering is better for annotation, we can always merge clusters later on if needed. We will choose resolution 0.32 for downstream analysis.

```{r CD4 Choose opti res}
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- FindClusters(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, graph.name = "wsnn", resolution = 0.32, verbose = FALSE)

# Choose resolution 0.06
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters <- seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$wsnn_res.0.32

# Reorganize clusters names
seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters <- factor(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters, 
                              levels = names(sort(table(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters), 
                                                  decreasing = TRUE)))
```

### Clusters on UMAP {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Clusters on UMAP}

  filename = file.path(

    "CD4_Tcells",

  height = 6)

    DIR_RESULT,

  width = 12,
  height = 6)

  scale_color_manual(values = pal) +
```

### Clusters quality {.tabset}

#### QC metrics per cluster {.tabset}

```{r CD4 QC metrics per clusters}
VlnPlot(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"),
  cols = pal,
  group.by = "seurat_clusters",
  pt.size = 0,
  ncol = 3)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Clustering",
    "Clusters_QC_metrics.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

#### Cell counts per cluster {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Cell counts per clusters}
# Plot number of cells per cluster with percentage labels of total cells
cell_counts <- seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data %>%
  count(seurat_clusters, name = "Cell_Count") %>%
  mutate(
    Percentage = round(100 * Cell_Count / sum(Cell_Count), 6),
    label = paste0(Percentage, "%")
  )


ggplot(cell_counts, aes(y = seurat_clusters, x = Cell_Count, fill = seurat_clusters)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Number (and %) of cells per cluster",
    x = "Number of cells",
    y = "Cluster"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal)

# Save the plot
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Clustering",
    "Clusters_cell_counts.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

#### Cluster repartition per patients {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Clusters repartition per patients}
# Proportion of cells per cluster for each patient
cluster_patient <- seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data %>%
  group_by(id, seurat_clusters) %>%
  summarise(Cell_Count = n()) %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = Cell_Count / sum(Cell_Count) * 100)

ggplot(cluster_patient, aes(x = id, y = Percentage, fill = seurat_clusters)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Proportion of cells per cluster for each patient",
    x = "Patient ID",
    y = "Proportion of cells",
    fill = "Cluster"
  ) + 
  theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, 
                                 hjust = 1, 
                                 vjust = 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal)

# Save the plot
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Clustering",
    "Clusters_repartition_per_patient.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

There are some clusters that are enriched in specific patients, which could be due to biological differences between patients (e.g., different disease states, immune responses) or technical variation (e.g., batch effects). It's important to investigate these clusters further to determine if they represent biologically meaningful subpopulations of CD4 T cells or if they are artifacts of the data. This can be done by looking at the expression of marker genes, performing differential expression analysis, and considering the clinical metadata associated with each patient.

#### Mitochondrial content per cluster {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Mito per clusters}
stats_mt <- seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data %>%
  group_by(seurat_clusters) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_pct_mt   = mean(percent.mt),
    median_pct_mt = median(percent.mt),
    sd_pct_mt     = sd(percent.mt)
  )

kable(stats_mt, format = "html") %>%
  kable_styling()

# Save the table
write.table(stats_mt, 
            file = file.path(
              DIR_RESULT,
              "CD4_Tcells",
              "Clustering",
              "cluster_mitochondrial_content_stats.tsv"), 
            row.names = FALSE, 
            sep = "\t")
```

```{r CD4 Viz Mito per clusters}
ggplot(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data,
       aes(
         x = percent.mt,
         y = seurat_clusters,
         fill = seurat_clusters
       )) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    alpha = 0.7,
    scale = 1.2,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    color = "white") +
  # theme_ridges() +
  # theme(
  #   legend.position = "none"
  # ) +
  # geom_vline(xintercept = 20, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    x = "% mitochondrial genes",
    y = "Clusters") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Clustering",
    "Clusters_mitochondrial_content_ridges.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

#### Cell cycle phase per cluster {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Cell cycle phase per clusters}
df <- seu_CD4_QC_Moderate@meta.data %>%
  group_by(seurat_clusters, Phase) %>%
  summarise(n = n()) %>%
  mutate(freq = n / sum(n))

ggplot(df, aes(x = seurat_clusters, y = freq, fill = Phase)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(x = "Cluster", y = "Proportion of cells", fill = "Cell cycle phase") +
  theme_minimal() +
  ggtitle("Distribution of cell cycle phases per cluster") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Clustering",
    "Clusters_cell_cycle_phase_distribution.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)

ggplot(df, aes(x = seurat_clusters, y = n, fill = Phase)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(x = "Cluster", y = "Number of cells", fill = "Cell cycle phase") +
  theme_minimal() +
  ggtitle("Cell counts per cell cycle phase and cluster") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Clustering",
    "Clusters_cell_cycle_phase_counts.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)

mat <- df %>%
  select(seurat_clusters, Phase, freq) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Phase, values_from = freq, values_fill = 0) %>%
  column_to_rownames("seurat_clusters")

pheatmap(as.matrix(mat),
         cluster_rows = TRUE,
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         main = "Cell cycle phase proportions per cluster")

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Clustering",
    "Clusters_cell_cycle_phase_heatmap.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)

DimPlot(
  seu_CD4_QC_Moderate,
  reduction = "umap",
  group.by = "seurat_clusters",
  split.by = "Phase",
  cols = pal
) + ggtitle("UMAP colored by cell cycle phase")

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD4_Tcells",
    "Clustering",
    "UMAP_seurat_clusters_split_by_cell_cycle_phase.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

### Remove bad clusters {.tabset}

```{r CD4 Remove bad clusters}
# Remove clusters 
# seu_CD4_QC_Moderate <- subset(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate, 
#               subset = !(seurat_clusters %in% c("8", "9","10")))
# 
# seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters <- factor(seu_CD4_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters)
```

I don't see any cluster to remove based on QC metrics, cell cycle phase distribution, and patient distribution. All clusters seem to have a reasonable number of cells, a mix of cell cycle phases. Therefore, I will keep all clusters for downstream analyses. Maybe I will fuse some of them later based on their similarity in terms of marker gene expression and biological interpretation, but for now I will keep them separate to preserve the granularity of the data.

